name: Crockpot Rice Pudding with Fruit
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Anne Merrill
Preparation Time: 3:00
Use half nonfat and half whole milk, or all nonfat for lower fat content.
Substitute canned milk for the regular milk for a very rich flavor. The cooking time will vary greatly, anywhere from 1-1/2 to 3-1/2 hours. The longer it cooks, the thicker it will be.
It is important to have the dried apricots minced. Combine all ingredients in the crockpot. Stir to blend well. Cover and cook on high for 1-1/2 hours, or cook on high for the first 30 minutes, then turn to low and cook as long as desired. Check after the first 2 hours of low cooking and stir. If rice is not absorbing the milk quickly enough, return the crockpot to high heat.
Crockpot temperatures vary widely among different brands. Only experimentation can determine the correct amount of time for cooking in your crockpot. Rarely will a crockpot recipe fail, as the slow, long cooking process does not require precise timing.
